title: Phyllagathis rufa
taxonomy: Phyllagathis rufa is classified within the family Melastomataceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family is part of the order Myrtales, which comprises various flowering plant families. The order Myrtales is a member of the clade Rosidae, a large group of flowering plant families. The clade Rosidae is part of the larger clade Eurosidae, which includes a wide range of flowering plant families. This classification indicates that Phyllagathis rufa is a flowering plant with a specific evolutionary history.

The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
